question: What triggers a change detection cycle in Angular's Default strategy?
options: Only when @Input values change
Any async event patched by Zone.js (setTimeout, fetch, DOM events, etc.)
Only when the component emits an EventEmitter
Only when the developer calls detectChanges() manually

question: Which conditions cause a component with OnPush strategy to be checked? (Best answer)
options: Only when the parent component re-renders
Only when an @Input reference changes
When an @Input reference changes, an async pipe emits, an event originates from the component, or markForCheck() is called
When any property on the component instance changes, regardless of reference

question: You have an OnPush component. You call `this.users.push(newUser)` inside the component. What happens?
options: The template updates immediately because the array now has a new item
The template does not update because the array reference did not change
Angular throws a runtime error for mutating an array
The template updates on the next setTimeout tick

question: What is the difference between ChangeDetectorRef.markForCheck() and ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges()?
options: markForCheck() runs synchronously; detectChanges() schedules for the next frame
markForCheck() marks the component as dirty for the next cycle; detectChanges() runs the check immediately and synchronously
They are identical; both schedule an async check
markForCheck() only works with Default strategy; detectChanges() only works with OnPush

question: How do Angular Signals relate to Zone.js and change detection?
options: Signals require Zone.js to notify Angular of changes
Signals work only with the Default change detection strategy
Signals bypass Zone.js entirely — Angular tracks which template bindings depend on each signal and updates only those bindings when the signal changes
Signals are a replacement for @Input and have nothing to do with change detection
